topics.name: 2 Thessalonians
topics.description: Posts on 2 Thessalonians, including authorship, forgery, apocalyptic expectation, further reading, and later interpretation.

topics.name: Academic Careers and University Life
topics.description: Covers graduate study, teaching, dissertations, university life, scholarly careers, and what academic biblical scholars actually do.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate

topics.name: Scholarly Research and Publishing
topics.description: Covers scholarly research projects, academic publishing, peer review, journals, reference works, editorial work, and the professional production of scholarship.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate

topics.name: Acts
topics.description: Posts on the book of Acts, including its literary features, historical reliability, major topics, theology, and account of early Christianity's spread.

topics.name: Life After Death (General)
topics.description: Covers broad questions about life after death, including death, consciousness, reincarnation, animals, religious belief, and changing afterlife beliefs.

topics.name: Agnosticism
topics.description: Covers agnosticism, including Bart's own views, interviews, personal reflections, uncertainty about God, and the difference between agnosticism and atheism.

topics.name: Ancient Jewish Afterlife Beliefs
topics.description: Covers ancient Jewish views of death and life after death, including Sheol, Ecclesiastes, Samuel, Enoch, resurrection, and disagreements among Jewish groups.

topics.name: Ancient Literacy
topics.description: Posts on reading, writing, literacy rates, education, secretaries, and whether figures such as Jesus, Peter, or early Christians could read or write.

topics.name: Ancient Miracle Workers
topics.description: Covers ancient figures associated with miracle-working, including Vespasian, Jewish miracle workers, Jesus as a wonder-worker, and early Christian apologetic claims.

topics.name: Ancient Secretaries and Authorship
topics.description: Covers ancient secretarial practices and how they affect authorship claims, including whether secretaries could compose texts, explain differences in style, or account for writings attributed to apostles.

topics.name: Wealth and Poverty in Antiquity
topics.description: Covers ancient views of wealth, poverty, charity, contentment, Cynic poverty, and moral arguments about possessions.

topics.name: Christian Anti-Judaism
topics.description: Covers anti-Jewish ideas and interpretations in Christian texts and traditions, including portrayals of Jews, Pilate, scripture, and later Christian rhetoric.

topics.name: Apocalypse of Peter
topics.description: Covers the Apocalypse of Peter, including its afterlife scenes, relationship to heaven and hell, canon debates, Christology, and why it was not included in the New Testament.

topics.name: Apocalyptic Jesus
topics.description: Posts on Jesus as a Jewish apocalyptic teacher, including his proclamation of God's coming kingdom, judgment, the Son of Man, messiahship, and later reinterpretations of his message.

topics.name: Apocryphal Acts
topics.description: Covers apocryphal acts attributed to apostles, including legendary missionary stories, miracle traditions, chastity, wealth, and traditions about Peter, Paul, Thomas, and other apostles.

topics.name: Apostolic Death Traditions
topics.description: Covers traditions about the deaths of Peter, Paul, and other apostles, including martyrdom claims and how those stories are used in later arguments.

topics.name: Apostolic Fathers
topics.description: Posts on early Christian writings and figures traditionally grouped as the Apostolic Fathers, including texts near the period after the New Testament.

topics.name: Apostolic Fathers Attribution
topics.description: Posts on authorship and attribution of early Christian writings such as 1 Clement, the Shepherd of Hermas, and the Letter of Barnabas.

topics.name: Archaeology and Material Evidence
topics.description: Posts on archaeological evidence and material remains relevant to Jesus, early Judaism, and early Christianity, including Nazareth, Capernaum, crucifixion nails, artifacts, and the limits of archaeological claims.

topics.name: Arius and Arianism
topics.description: Covers Arius, the Arian controversy, and debates over Christ's divine status around the Council of Nicaea.

topics.name: Armageddon
topics.description: Posts about Bart's book Armageddon, including announcements, interviews, excerpts, publication updates, and discussions of Revelation or modern end-times interpretation tied to the book.

topics.name: Athanasius of Alexandria
topics.description: Covers Athanasius, especially his role in canon formation, Nicene Christianity, and fourth-century theological debates.

topics.name: Atheism
topics.description: Covers atheism, including why people do or do not believe in God, atheist engagement with Jesus and the Bible, and relationships between believers and nonbelievers.

topics.name: Atonement (General)
topics.description: Covers Jesus' death as atonement, sacrifice, forgiveness, vicarious suffering, or the means of salvation outside the Luke-Acts-specific discussion.

topics.name: Augustine of Hippo
topics.description: Covers Augustine's writings, theology, conversion, views of miracles, ethics, and influence on later Christianity.

topics.name: Autobiographical Posts
topics.description: Posts about Bart's life, education, career, family memories, retirement, and personal experiences.

topics.name: Biblical Debates on Same-Sex Relations
topics.description: Posts on biblical passages, translation issues, webinars, and interpretive debates about same-sex relations and sexual morality.

topics.name: Bible Translations (General)
topics.description: Covers broad questions about Bible translations, including choosing translations, comparing versions, the Apocrypha, and how translations relate to manuscripts and interpretation.

topics.name: Biblical Contradictions
topics.description: Posts on contradictions in biblical texts, including the Gospels, the Hebrew Bible, and broader questions of interpretation and scriptural authority.

topics.name: Biblical Inerrancy
topics.description: Posts on claims that the Bible is without error, including fundamentalism, contradictions, personal deconversion, and debates over biblical authority.

topics.name: Biblical Numerology
topics.description: Covers number symbolism and numerical interpretation in biblical texts, especially 666, gematria, Greek numerals, and Matthew's genealogy.

topics.name: Biblical Explanations of Suffering
topics.description: Covers how biblical texts explain suffering, including divine punishment, wisdom traditions, Job, Ecclesiastes, and competing biblical answers.

topics.name: Jesus' Birth Narratives
topics.description: Covers stories of Jesus' birth in Matthew and Luke, including Bethlehem, Herod, the census, angels, contradictions, and questions of history and theology.

topics.name: Blog Updates and Fundraising
topics.description: Posts about blog anniversaries, year-in-review updates, fundraising appeals, charity campaigns, stewardship, and support for charitable work.

topics.name: Bloody Sweat Textual Variant
topics.description: Posts on the textual variant in Luke where Jesus sweats blood, including manuscript evidence, scribal motives, NRSV translation issues, and Luke's portrayal of Jesus before death.

topics.name: Bruce Metzger
topics.description: Covers Bruce Metzger as Bart's teacher and mentor, including graduate training, dissertation work, textual criticism, Bible translation, and personal reminiscences.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate

topics.name: Burial of Jesus
topics.description: Covers whether Jesus was buried, who may have buried him, Jewish and Roman burial practices, Josephus, Pilate, archaeological evidence, and the historical plausibility of the burial traditions.

topics.name: Canon Formation
topics.description: Posts on how writings became scripture, including early canon lists, disputed books, books included or excluded, and the formation of the New Testament and Hebrew Bible canons.

topics.name: Canonical Gospels (General)
topics.description: Posts on broad questions involving the canonical Gospels as a group, including their sources, shared traditions, relationships to one another, differences in presentation, and general interpretation.

topics.name: Celsus
topics.description: Posts on Celsus as an ancient critic of Christianity, including his comments on Jesus, Christian origins, and early pagan objections to Christian claims.

topics.name: Charity and Altruism
topics.description: Posts on charity, altruism, giving, care for the poor, and debates over moral obligations to help those in need.

topics.name: Angelic Christology
topics.description: Posts on angelic Christology, especially whether early Christians, including Paul, understood Christ as an angelic or preexistent heavenly being.

topics.name: Christian Interpretation of Jewish Scripture
topics.description: Covers how early Christians interpreted Jewish scripture, used prophetic texts, debated meaning, and developed Christian readings of Israel's scriptures.

topics.name: Christology in the Gospels
topics.description: Covers how Gospel texts portray Jesus' identity, sonship, divinity, messiahship, and relationship to God.

topics.name: Comparative Ancient Evidence
topics.description: Covers comparisons between biblical texts, historical claims, manuscript traditions, miracle stories, creation accounts, ethics, and other ancient or Greco-Roman materials.

topics.name: Codex Sinaiticus
topics.description: Covers Codex Sinaiticus, including its discovery, St. Catherine's Monastery, its importance for the New Testament and Septuagint, and Bart's visits or reflections.

topics.name: Constantine
topics.description: Posts on Constantine's conversion, rule, relationship to Christianity, role in church history, and connection with Nicaea and the Roman Empire.

topics.name: Conversion
topics.description: Covers why people converted to Christianity, including miracles, fear of hell, social appeal, martyrdom, missions, and ancient accounts of conversion.

topics.name: Critical Biblical Scholarship
topics.description: Covers critical biblical scholarship, scholarly consensus, seminary training, and how academic study differs from devotional or fundamentalist approaches.

topics.name: Crucified Messiah
topics.description: Covers why a crucified messiah was a problem, how early Christians interpreted Jesus' crucifixion, and how messiahship, kingship, Judaism, and Paul shape the issue.

topics.name: Crucifixion of Jesus
topics.description: Covers Jesus' crucifixion as a historical event, including how it is reconstructed, disputed, interpreted, or compared with later religious claims.

topics.name: Dating Ancient Texts
topics.description: Covers how scholars date ancient writings, including the Gospels, Barnabas, and other early Christian texts.

topics.name: Dead Sea Scrolls and Essenes
topics.description: Covers the Dead Sea Scrolls, the Essenes, Qumran, Jewish sectarian movements, manuscript discoveries, and their significance for early Judaism and Jesus.

topics.name: Public Debates
topics.description: Covers public debates, debate strategy, announcements, rebuttals, and exchanges over biblical history, reliability, miracles, suffering, and faith.

topics.name: Deconversion
topics.description: Covers leaving Christian faith, including Bart's own loss of faith, pastors who lose faith, family and community effects, inerrancy, suffering, and reasons for no longer being Christian.

topics.name: Demythologizing the New Testament
topics.description: Covers demythologizing the New Testament, including arguments for and against reinterpreting supernatural or ethical claims in modern terms.

topics.name: Didache
topics.description: Covers the Didache, including prayers, church order, ethical instructions, dating, composition, and its place among early Christian writings.

topics.name: Did Jesus Exist?
topics.description: Posts on Bart's book Did Jesus Exist?, including announcements, interviews, video presentations, reader questions, and responses to mythicist criticism.

topics.name: Didymus the Blind
topics.description: Posts on Didymus the Blind, especially his writings, textual evidence, and connection to Bart's scholarly work.

topics.name: Disasters and Human Suffering
topics.description: Covers natural disasters, large-scale suffering, and attempts to explain tragedy through theology, apocalypticism, or divine action.

topics.name: Divine Beings in the Hebrew Bible
topics.description: Covers angels, divine figures, heavenly beings, monsters, monotheism, personified Wisdom, and how ancient Israelites and Jews understood God and other powers.

topics.name: Divine Judgment
topics.description: Covers God's judgment, punishment, wrath, destruction, justice, and biblical claims about accountability before God.

topics.name: Early Christian Diversity
topics.description: Covers competing forms of early Christianity, including alternative beliefs, rival groups, unorthodox texts, and the question of which Christianity became dominant.

topics.name: Early Christian Teachings on Wealth
topics.description: Posts on early Christian teachings about wealth, poverty, giving, and possessions, especially in texts such as the Shepherd of Hermas and the Didache.

topics.name: Early Christian Writings
topics.description: Covers early Christian writings outside or alongside the New Testament, including collections, lost texts, dating, transmission, and later use.

topics.name: Early Christianity (General)
topics.description: Covers broad issues in early Christianity, including beliefs, practices, social setting, historical development, and the early Christian movement.

topics.name: Christology (General)
topics.description: Covers broad questions about Jesus' identity, divine status, relationship to God, and the Jewish or ancient background for later Christian claims about Christ.

topics.name: Early Church Developments
topics.description: Covers broad developments in post-New-Testament church history, including bishops, councils, institutional growth, Constantine, persecution, Christianization of Rome, and changing Christian practices.

topics.name: Early Judaism (General)
topics.description: Covers Jewish groups, practices, historical settings, and beliefs in the Second Temple period and around the time of Jesus.

topics.name: Ecclesiastes
topics.description: Covers Ecclesiastes, including mortality, meaning, wisdom, skepticism, suffering, and possible afterlife expectations.

topics.name: Empty Tomb Traditions
topics.description: Covers empty tomb traditions, including the women at the tomb, invention arguments, doubt, burial questions, and connections with resurrection appearances.

topics.name: Eternal Punishment
topics.description: Covers hell, Gehenna, eternal torment, annihilation, the unforgivable sin, degrees of punishment, and Christian debates about divine justice.

topics.description: Covers Eusebius as an early church historian and source for Constantine, heresy, canon, and early Christian history.

topics.description: Posts on views that Jesus became or was declared God's Son, Lord, or exalted figure at resurrection, baptism, or another decisive moment.

topics.name: Adoptionist Christology
topics.description: Posts on adoptionist Christology, especially views that Jesus was adopted, appointed, or declared God's Son at baptism, resurrection, or another point.

topics.name: Eyewitness Reliability
topics.description: Covers the reliability and limits of eyewitness testimony, including memory distortion, expertise, oral cultures, and whether eyewitnesses preserve historical events accurately.

topics.name: First-Century Mark Fragment
topics.description: Covers the claimed first-century fragment of Mark, including mummy masks, discovery claims, scholarly controversy, and whether such a fragment would change what we know.

topics.name: Forged
topics.description: Posts about Bart's trade book on ancient Christian literary forgery, including announcements, excerpts, interviews, and discussions of authorship and deception.

topics.description: Covers ancient literary forgery, pseudepigraphy, deception, motives for false attribution, and general debates about whether forged writings were meant to mislead readers.

topics.name: Forgery and Counterforgery
topics.description: Posts about Bart's scholarly study of pseudepigraphy, deception, and disputes over forged writings in early Christianity.

topics.name: Free Will and Predestination
topics.description: Covers free will and predestination, including divine sovereignty, human choice, salvation, suffering, and whether people can lose salvation.

topics.name: Free Will Explanations of Suffering
topics.description: Covers whether free will explains suffering, including critiques of free-will defenses and related arguments about natural evil.

topics.name: Fundamentalism
topics.description: Covers fundamentalist Christianity, including biblical inerrancy, evangelical apologetics, Bart's early religious background, and critiques of fundamentalist approaches to the Bible.

topics.name: General-Audience Books
topics.description: Covers books written for general readers, including trade books, book ideas, excerpts, publication plans, and efforts to make scholarship accessible.

topics.name: Genesis
topics.description: Covers Genesis, including creation stories, Abraham, unusual biblical episodes, translation issues, source questions, and interpretation.

topics.name: Gnostic and Orthodox Conflicts
topics.description: Covers disputes between Gnostic or other non-orthodox Christians and emerging proto-orthodox Christianity, including scripture, doctrine, heresiology, and competing claims to truth.

topics.description: Covers general discussions of Gnosticism, including definitions, introductory summaries, classification problems, Nag Hammadi evidence, and examples of Gnostic or Gnostic-adjacent groups.

topics.description: Posts on who wrote the canonical Gospels, why they are anonymous, how titles and traditions developed, and how early Christians connected them with apostolic figures.

topics.description: Covers when the canonical Gospels were written, including arguments for post-70 dates and how scholars date the Gospels.

topics.description: Covers claims that the Gospels are based on eyewitness testimony, including Papias, Gospel authorship, Richard Bauckham-style arguments, and whether eyewitness access makes the Gospels reliable.

topics.description: Covers the historical reliability of the canonical Gospels, including how scholars evaluate claims, contradictions, memory, sources, and apologetic arguments.

topics.name: Gospel of Jesus' Wife Fragment
topics.description: Covers the Gospel of Jesus' Wife fragment, including discovery claims, forgery debates, media coverage, and questions about whether Jesus was married.

topics.description: Posts focused on the Gospel of John, including its authorship, distinctive theology, historical reliability, signs, symbolism, and portrayal of Jesus.

topics.description: Posts focused on the Gospel of Mark, including Markan priority, the Messianic Secret, textual issues, the portrayal of Jesus, and distinctive narrative features.

topics.name: Gospel of Matthew
topics.description: Posts focused on the Gospel of Matthew, including its use of Jewish scripture, genealogy, birth narrative, relation to Mark and Luke, portrayal of Jesus, and distinctive teachings.

topics.name: Gospel of Peter
topics.description: Covers the Gospel of Peter, including its surviving fragment, resurrection scene, connection to Peter, canon questions, and distinctive portrayal of Jesus' death.

topics.name: Gospel of Thomas
topics.description: Covers the Gospel of Thomas, including its sayings, possible relation to Q, use of canonical Gospels, Thomasine Christianity, and debates over its date and meaning.

topics.name: Greco-Roman Religious Culture
topics.description: Covers pagan worship, mystery religions, emperor worship, moral philosophy, virtue, benefaction, and the religious culture in which early Christianity developed.

topics.name: Christian Health Care
topics.description: Posts on Christian care for the sick, hospitals, and health care as possible factors in Christianity's spread.

topics.name: Heaven and Hell
topics.description: Posts about Bart's book on the development of Christian afterlife beliefs, including excerpts, interviews, reader responses, and related discussions.

topics.name: Heaven and Hell Beliefs
topics.description: Covers ancient and Christian ideas about heaven and hell, including reward, punishment, moral reversal, and connections with Jesus, Paul, Revelation, Jewish traditions, Greek traditions, and Roman traditions.

topics.name: Hebrew Bible Historical Reliability
topics.description: Covers historical questions about Hebrew Bible narratives, including Moses, Exodus, David, conquest, Israelite origins, miracle stories, archaeology, and comparative evidence.

topics.name: Historical Jesus (General)
topics.description: Posts offering broad overviews of the historical Jesus, including what can be known about his life, how scholars reconstruct it, and major debates about his significance.

topics.name: Methods for Studying the Historical Jesus
topics.description: Covers methods scholars use to evaluate what Jesus said and did, including criteria, evidence, apocalyptic interpretation, and historical plausibility.

topics.name: Historical Methods (General)
topics.description: Covers historical reasoning, evidence, genre, miracle claims, critical inquiry, and how historians approach the New Testament and other ancient sources.

topics.name: Historical Study of Miracles
topics.description: Covers how historians evaluate miracle claims, including the limits of historical method, probability, evidence, and supernatural explanations.

topics.description: Covers the relationship between historical study and theological claims, including whether theological truth depends on historical accuracy.

topics.name: Holy Spirit in Luke-Acts
topics.description: Posts on the Holy Spirit in Luke-Acts, including Jesus' empowerment by the Spirit and Pentecost in Acts.

topics.name: How Jesus Became God
topics.description: Posts about Bart's book on early Christology, Jesus' divinity, public debates, interviews, and related arguments.

topics.name: Ignore
topics.description: Administrative, announcement, or housekeeping posts not intended for topical browsing.

topics.name: In a Nutshell Series
topics.description: Brief overview posts that summarize texts, movements, people, or historical issues in a compact In a Nutshell format.

topics.description: Posts on incarnation Christology, especially views that Christ was a preexistent divine or heavenly being who became human.

topics.description: Covers the Infancy Gospel of Thomas, including stories of Jesus as a child, miracle traditions, interpretive problems, and relation to other infancy traditions.

topics.description: Covers the Edessa/Abgar tradition, especially stories that Jesus wrote a letter and later claims about writings attributed to Jesus.

topics.description: Covers Jesus' teachings, parables, and historical setting in relation to wealth, poverty, possessions, economic survival, and social class.

topics.description: Covers women in Jesus' life, teaching, social world, and Gospel traditions, including Mary Magdalene and women followers or patrons.

topics.name: Jesus Before the Gospels
topics.description: Posts about memory, oral tradition, eyewitness claims, Gospel transmission, and public discussions connected with Bart's book.

topics.description: Covers Jesus' moral teachings, Jewish roots of his ethics, altruism, wealth, love of neighbor, and modern relevance.

topics.name: Jesus' Family Traditions
topics.description: Covers traditions and claims about Jesus' relatives, including Mary, his brothers, possible father, possible marriage, and later stories about his family.

topics.name: Genealogies of Jesus
topics.description: Covers the genealogies of Jesus in Matthew and Luke, including differences between them, women in Matthew's genealogy, textual problems, and theological aims.

topics.name: Jesus' Miracle Stories
topics.description: Covers miracle stories connected with Jesus, including healings, exorcisms, nature miracles, Gospel summaries, and historical questions about miracle claims.

topics.name: Jesus' Teachings
topics.description: Covers Jesus' teachings, especially parables, sayings, kingdom preaching, ethics, and how Gospel traditions present his message.

topics.name: Jewish Apocalypticism
topics.description: Covers ancient Jewish apocalyptic thought, including prophecy, historical crisis, apocalypse as a genre, resurrection, judgment, and its background for Jesus and early Christianity.

topics.name: Jewish Christian Gospels
topics.description: Covers Jewish-Christian gospels, including the Gospel of the Ebionites and other apocryphal gospels connected with Jewish-Christian groups.

topics.description: Covers Jewish law and Torah observance in biblical, Second Temple, Gospel, and Pauline contexts, including Matthew, Galatians, and debates over whether Judaism was legalistic.

topics.name: Book of Job
topics.description: Covers Job, including suffering, divine justice, Job's friends, the book's different perspectives, and biblical responses to pain.

topics.name: John the Baptist
topics.description: Covers John the Baptist, including his relationship to Jesus, baptism, apocalyptic preaching, Gospel traditions, birth narratives, and historical significance.

topics.name: Judas Iscariot
topics.description: Covers Judas Iscariot, including his betrayal of Jesus, death traditions, Gospel portrayals, Papias, and questions about what Paul or early Christians knew about him.

topics.name: King James Version
topics.description: Covers the King James Version, including translation choices, textual basis, famous typos, relation to Tyndale, and passages such as the ending of Mark.

topics.name: Letter of Barnabas
topics.description: Covers the Letter of Barnabas, including its attribution, date, use of scripture, gematria, and place among early Christian writings.

topics.name: Life of Brian
topics.description: Posts using Monty Python's Life of Brian to explore the historical Jesus, apocalypticism, Roman rule, burial, and public teaching.

topics.name: Atonement in Luke-Acts
topics.description: Covers whether Luke and Acts present Jesus' death as atoning, salvific, or significant in a different way, including Luke's Last Supper textual problem.

topics.name: Luke-Acts Authorship
topics.description: Posts on who wrote Luke and Acts, whether the author was Paul's companion Luke, and how the two-volume work identifies or conceals its author.

topics.name: Manuscript Dating
topics.description: Covers how scholars date manuscripts, including paleography, radiocarbon dating, mummy masks, Gospel fragments, Quran fragments, and questions about original manuscripts.

topics.description: Covers manuscript discoveries and controversies, including mummy masks, Quran fragments, the Didache, new finds, acquisition ethics, and scholarly issues around ancient manuscripts.

topics.name: Marcion
topics.description: Covers Marcion, his teachings, canon, view of Jewish scriptures, relationship to Paul, and later Christian judgments of his theology.

topics.name: Martyrdom Traditions (General)
topics.description: Covers martyrdom as a broad topic, including the value assigned to martyrdom, suffering, conversion, martyr texts, and the memory of the dead.

topics.name: Martyrdom of Perpetua
topics.description: Covers the Martyrdom of Perpetua, especially its afterlife imagery, family conflict, suffering, and later significance for ideas such as purgatory.

topics.name: Martyrdom of Polycarp
topics.description: Covers the Martyrdom of Polycarp, including its claims, literary features, martyrdom theology, miracles, and possible forgery.

topics.name: Mary Magdalene
topics.description: Covers Mary Magdalene, including her role among Jesus' followers, resurrection traditions, later legends, claims that she was a prostitute, and non-canonical traditions about her.

topics.name: Mary Magdalene in Gnostic Traditions
topics.description: Covers Mary Magdalene in Gnostic or non-canonical traditions, especially the Gospel of Philip, Gospel of Mary, and claims about Jesus' relationship to Mary.

topics.name: Matthew's Use of Scripture
topics.description: Posts on Matthew's fulfillment citations and how Matthew presents Jesus as fulfilling scripture.

topics.name: Media Coverage and Reviews
topics.description: Covers news coverage, reviews, bestseller lists, public controversy, and media attention involving Bart's books or topics from the blog.

topics.name: Media Interviews and Videos
topics.description: Covers interviews, podcasts, videos, broadcasts, documentaries, and other public media appearances.

topics.name: Memory and Jesus Traditions
topics.description: Covers memory, false memory, group memory, distortion, and how remembered traditions about Jesus may have changed over time.

topics.name: Messiah
topics.description: Posts on whether Jesus understood himself or was understood by others as Messiah, including Jewish messianic expectations, resurrection belief, and early Christian claims.

topics.name: Messianic Secret
topics.description: Posts on Mark's motif of Jesus' hidden or silenced identity, including why Mark presents Jesus telling others not to reveal who he is.

topics.name: Miracle Claims and Apologetics
topics.description: Covers miracle claims used in apologetic arguments, including eyewitness claims, signs from God, Augustine's miracles, resurrection arguments, and modern defenses of supernatural events.

topics.name: Miracle Traditions (General)
topics.description: Covers miracles as a broad topic, including divine action, miracle traditions, suffering, faith, and unusual claims that do not fit a narrower miracle topic.

topics.name: Miraculous Martyrdom Traditions
topics.description: Covers miraculous elements in martyrdom traditions, including painless torture, Polycarp, apostolic deaths, and Christian claims about suffering and divine power.

topics.name: Misquoting Jesus
topics.description: Posts about Bart's book Misquoting Jesus, including its argument, reception, critiques, interviews, and broader issues in textual criticism.

topics.name: Modern End-Times Interpretation
topics.description: Covers modern end-times interpretation, including the rapture, Armageddon, Israel, Waco, American politics, and readings of biblical prophecy as predictions of current events.

topics.name: Modern Forgery Claims
topics.description: Covers modern forgery claims and disputed discoveries, including the Gospel of Jesus' Wife, Secret Gospel of Mark, Museum of the Bible controversies, and other recent authenticity debates.

topics.name: Moral Philosophy
topics.description: Covers ethical reasoning, altruism, lying, war, Stoic moral thought, happiness, contentment, and broader questions about how people should live.

topics.name: Moral Problems in Scripture
topics.description: Covers ethical difficulties in biblical texts, including slavery, violence, divine judgment, gender, and attempts to defend or reinterpret troubling passages.

topics.name: Mythicism
topics.description: Posts on arguments that Jesus did not exist, Bart's responses to mythicists, evidence for Jesus' existence, and related debates about Nazareth or Jesus' family.

topics.name: Nag Hammadi
topics.description: Covers the Nag Hammadi library, its discovery, contents, interpretation, and debates about whether its texts illuminate Gnostic Christianity.

topics.name: Nazareth
topics.description: Posts on Nazareth, including archaeological evidence, claims that it did not exist, Gospel references, and whether Jesus was called a Nazarene or from Nazareth.

topics.name: New Testament Manuscripts
topics.description: Covers Greek New Testament manuscripts, early papyri, manuscript evidence, amulets, copying, and what manuscripts can or cannot show about original texts.

topics.name: Non-Canonical Gospel Traditions
topics.description: Covers non-canonical gospel traditions outside the New Testament when no more specific topic is used, including lost, fragmentary, legendary, infancy, Pilate, and other gospel-like traditions.

topics.name: Miracle Stories in Non-Canonical Texts
topics.description: Covers miracle stories in non-canonical Christian texts, including Apocryphal Acts, Infancy Gospel of Thomas, Acts of Thomas, and stories about the young Jesus or apostles.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistle Forgeries
topics.description: Covers forged or pseudepigraphic non-Pauline letters, especially James, Jude, and the Johannine letters.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistles
topics.description: Covers Hebrews, James, 1-2 Peter, the Johannine letters, Jude, and other New Testament letters not attributed to Paul.

topics.name: Non-Pauline Epistle Authorship
topics.description: Posts on authorship questions in Hebrews, Jude, 1 Peter, the Johannine letters, and other New Testament epistles not attributed to Paul.

topics.name: New Revised Standard Version (NRSV)
topics.description: Covers the New Revised Standard Version, including Bart's work on the translation committee, inclusive language, translation decisions, and the role of Bruce Metzger.

topics.name: Old Testament Apocrypha
topics.description: Covers Jewish apocryphal writings outside the Protestant Old Testament, including Tobit, Judith, Sirach, Wisdom of Solomon, 1-2 Maccabees, additions to Daniel, and related texts.

topics.name: Oral Tradition
topics.description: Covers oral transmission of stories about Jesus, including storytelling, oral cultures, Papias, Gospel traditions before writing, and how traditions changed before becoming texts.

topics.name: Original Text Questions
topics.description: Posts on whether the original wording of New Testament writings can be identified, including lost originals, authorial drafts, copied texts, and arguments for reconstruction.

topics.name: Early Christian Orthodoxy and Heresy
topics.description: Covers debates over orthodoxy and heresy in early Christianity, including early Christian diversity, Bauer's thesis, Eusebius, Marcion, adoptionist views, Jewish-Christian texts, and the rise of proto-orthodox Christianity.

topics.name: Afterlife Journeys
topics.description: Covers ancient accounts of journeys to heaven, hell, Hades, or the realm of the dead, including Enoch, Paul, the Apocalypse of Peter, the Apocalypse of Paul, and Greco-Roman traditions.

topics.name: Papias
topics.description: Posts on Papias, especially his claims about Gospel origins, oral tradition, Matthew, Mark, and early Christian testimony.

topics.name: Papyrus Egerton
topics.description: Covers Papyrus Egerton, an early non-canonical gospel fragment, including its stories, relationship to other Gospel traditions, and significance for early Gospel circulation.

topics.name: Jesus' Passion Narratives
topics.description: Covers Gospel passion narratives about Jesus' final days, including the Last Supper, temple curtain, triumphal entry, blasphemy charge, Pilate, anti-Judaism, and narrative shaping.

topics.name: Church Fathers as Textual Evidence
topics.description: Posts on using quotations in early Christian writers to reconstruct the New Testament text, including the limits, dating, location, and reliability of this evidence.

topics.name: Paul on Jesus' Crucifixion
topics.description: Covers how Paul interprets Jesus' crucifixion, including the crucified messiah, Jesus' death, and the meaning of the cross in Paul's letters.

topics.name: Paul on Jewish Law
topics.description: Covers Paul's views of Jewish law, including Galatians, Gentile believers, salvation, love, and debates over law observance.

topics.name: Paul and His Opponents
topics.description: Covers conflicts involving Paul, including rival teachers, disputes with other apostles, opposition within Pauline churches, and later challenges to Paul's authority.

topics.name: Paul as Persecutor and Persecuted
topics.description: Covers Paul as both persecutor and persecuted figure, including Acts, Paul's letters, historical reconstruction, and claims about opposition to Paul.

topics.name: Paul on Resurrection
topics.description: Covers Paul's teaching about resurrection, including Jesus' resurrection, the resurrection of believers, spiritual bodies, and eternal life.

topics.name: Women in Pauline Traditions
topics.description: Covers women in Pauline traditions, including Paul's views of women, women associated with Paul, women apostles, interpolation debates, and gender roles in Pauline communities.

topics.name: Paul in Acts
topics.description: Covers Acts' portrayal of Paul, including conversion accounts, missionary work, speeches, chronology, and historical comparison with Paul's letters.

topics.name: Paul's Knowledge of Jesus
topics.description: Covers what Paul appears to know about Jesus, including Jesus' teachings, death, disciples, family, and traditions cited in Paul's letters.

topics.name: Pauline End-Time Expectations
topics.description: Covers Paul's end-time expectations, including Jesus' return, resurrection, the Spirit, the delay of the end, and later rapture interpretations of Paul's letters.

topics.name: Pauline Authorship
topics.description: Posts on which letters Paul wrote, which letters are disputed, and how scholars use style, theology, historical setting, and comparison with undisputed letters to assess authorship.

topics.name: Paul's Life and Career
topics.description: Covers Paul's biography, chronology, travel, imprisonment, letters, and the sources used to reconstruct his life and work.

topics.name: Paul's Churches and Communities
topics.description: Covers communities connected with Paul, including Corinth, Thessalonica, Gentile inclusion, internal conflict, and expectations about the end.

topics.name: Paul's Conversion
topics.description: Covers Paul's conversion, its historical setting, and how the experience shaped Paul's mission and understanding of Gentile inclusion.

topics.name: Paul and Slavery
topics.description: Covers Paul, Philemon, Onesimus, and slavery, especially whether Paul's letters challenge or accept slavery.

topics.name: Pauline Forgeries
topics.description: Covers letters falsely attributed to Paul, including disputed Pauline letters, pseudepigraphy, motives for forgery, and debates over authenticity.

topics.name: Composite Pauline Letters
topics.description: Covers arguments that some Pauline letters, especially 2 Corinthians and Philippians, may combine originally separate letters or fragments.

topics.name: Pauline Letters
topics.description: Posts on Paul's letters as a group, including lost letters, individual epistles, interpretation, communities, and broader questions about Paul's correspondence.

topics.name: Pauline Salvation Models
topics.description: Covers Paul's understandings of salvation, especially judicial and participationist models, grace, atonement, and the believer's relation to Christ.

topics.name: Pauline Textual Issues
topics.description: Posts on textual issues in Paul's letters, including variants, interpolations, scribal changes, manuscript evidence, dictation, and questions about the original text.

topics.name: Pentateuch
topics.description: Covers the Torah/Pentateuch, including Moses, Exodus, Sinai, law, contradictions, source theories, and historical questions about its narratives.

topics.name: Persecution in the Epistles
topics.description: Covers persecution, suffering, opposition, and endurance as addressed in New Testament letters such as 1 Peter, Hebrews, 1 Thessalonians, and 2 Thessalonians.

topics.name: Personal Reflections
topics.description: Posts offering personal reflections on belief, doubt, ethics, suffering, public issues, holidays, or life experience.

topics.name: Petrine Authorship and Forgeries
topics.description: Covers writings attributed to Peter, including debates over 1 Peter, 2 Peter, secretaries, literacy, authorship, and other texts falsely or questionably connected with Peter.

topics.name: Philippians
topics.description: Posts on Philippians, including authorship, composition, textual problems, further reading, and the letter's place among Paul's writings.

topics.name: Philippians 2 Christ Poem
topics.description: Covers Philippians 2:6-11, including Christ's status, humility, exaltation, and possible preexistence or incarnation.

topics.name: Pontius Pilate
topics.description: Covers Pontius Pilate, including his role in Jesus' trial and crucifixion, Gospel portrayals, anti-Jewish traditions, burial questions, and later legends about Pilate.

topics.name: Public-Facing Scholarship
topics.description: Covers efforts to explain biblical scholarship to wider audiences through articles, books, media, public responses, and accessible presentations of academic work.

topics.name: Problem of Evil and Suffering
topics.description: Covers suffering as a challenge to belief in a loving and all-powerful God, including debates, proposed answers, apologetic arguments, biblical responses, and personal reflections.

topics.name: Hebrew Bible Prophets
topics.description: Covers Hebrew Bible prophets, including Isaiah, Hosea, Zechariah, Daniel, prophecy, suffering servant traditions, and prophetic interpretation.

topics.name: Proto-Gospel of James
topics.description: Covers the Proto-Gospel of James, including traditions about Mary's background, Jesus' birth, Jesus' brothers, and later Christian infancy stories.

topics.name: Purgatory
topics.description: Posts on purgatory and related ideas, including postmortem purification, unforgivable sin, degrees of punishment, Perpetua, and the historical emergence of purgatorial belief.

topics.name: Q Source
topics.description: Posts on Q as a proposed source behind material shared by Matthew and Luke, including its reconstruction, possible contents, and significance for the Synoptic Problem.

topics.name: Quran Manuscript Fragments
topics.description: Covers ancient Quran manuscript fragments, including discovery claims, radiocarbon dating, manuscript dating, and broader issues in comparing Quranic and biblical manuscript evidence.

topics.name: Redaction Criticism
topics.description: Covers redaction criticism, especially how Gospel writers shaped, edited, and interpreted inherited traditions for their own theological or literary purposes.

topics.name: Resurrection Arguments and Apologetics
topics.description: Covers arguments for or against proving Jesus' resurrection, including debate claims, legal arguments, miracle reasoning, eyewitness claims, and apologetic defenses.

topics.name: Jesus' Resurrection Appearances
topics.description: Covers traditions that Jesus appeared after death, including appearances to disciples, women, Paul, the Twelve, the five hundred, and questions about visions or hallucinations.

topics.name: Resurrection of Jesus
topics.description: Covers Jesus' resurrection broadly, including historical reconstruction, early Christian belief, miracle claims, tomb traditions, and how the resurrection was understood.

topics.name: Resurrection of the Dead
topics.description: Posts on future bodily resurrection, including Jewish origins, Daniel, Maccabean martyrdom, Paul, physical embodiment, personal identity, and resurrection as afterlife hope.

topics.name: Meaning of Jesus' Resurrection
topics.description: Covers early Christian interpretations of Jesus' resurrection, including messiahship, salvation, the beginning of the church, Isaiah 53, Christology, and early Christian belief.

topics.name: Book of Revelation
topics.description: Covers the book of Revelation as an ancient apocalypse, including authorship, historical setting, symbolism, violence, judgment, 666, afterlife imagery, and modern misreadings.

topics.name: Revelation Authorship
topics.description: Covers who wrote Revelation, including the identity of John of Patmos and whether Revelation was written by the same author as the Gospel of John.

topics.name: Rise of Christianity
topics.description: Covers how Christianity spread and became successful in the Roman world, including social, religious, political, and cultural explanations.

topics.name: Roman Crucifixion and Burial
topics.description: Covers Roman crucifixion and burial practices, including how crucifixion was carried out, whether victims were buried, and how this evidence relates to Jesus' burial.

topics.name: Roman Persecution of Christians
topics.description: Covers Roman actions against Christians, including imperial involvement, accusations against Christians, Roman religious expectations, and the political context of persecution.

topics.name: Roman World
topics.description: Covers Roman political, legal, military, and cultural settings relevant to Jesus, Paul, early Judaism, early Christianity, and the spread of the Christian movement.

topics.name: Salvation (General)
topics.description: Covers who is saved, how salvation happens, whether salvation can be lost, and how Jesus, Paul, or later Christians understood eternal life.

topics.name: Scholar Tributes and Memorials
topics.description: Covers memorials and tributes to scholars and public religious figures, including their work, influence, and significance for biblical or early Christian studies.
categories: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate

topics.name: Scribal Changes
topics.description: Posts on how scribes altered manuscripts, including accidental mistakes, intentional changes, theological edits, harmonizations, and the historical significance of copying.

topics.name: Septuagint
topics.description: Covers the Greek translation of Jewish scripture, including its origin, textual issues, translation traditions, and importance for early Jewish and Christian interpretation.

topics.name: Sethian Gnostics
topics.description: Covers Sethian Gnostics, including their myths, teachings, relationship to the Gospel of Judas, and place within discussions of Gnostic Christianity.

topics.name: Sexual and Reproductive Ethics
topics.description: Posts on biblical and modern debates about sexuality, reproduction, surrogacy, gender identity, premarital sex, and the status of the unborn.

topics.name: Signs in the Gospel of John
topics.description: Covers the signs in the Gospel of John, including how John portrays Jesus' miracles, how John differs from the Synoptics, and whether signs function as evidence for Jesus' identity.

topics.name: Social Class in Antiquity
topics.description: Posts on class, status, labor, poverty, slavery, gender hierarchy, and social conditions in the ancient Mediterranean world.

topics.name: Son of Man
topics.description: Posts on the Son of Man sayings, including what the phrase meant, whether Jesus used it for himself, and its role in apocalyptic traditions.

topics.name: Source Criticism
topics.description: Covers source criticism, including how scholars identify written sources, composite texts, Gospel relationships, and possible spliced or edited writings.

topics.name: Suffering and Loss of Faith
topics.description: Covers suffering as a reason for losing or questioning faith, including personal reflections, disasters, miracles, and the problem of reconciling suffering with Christian belief.

topics.name: Synoptic Problem
topics.description: Posts on the literary relationships among Matthew, Mark, and Luke, including Markan priority, Q, source theories, and explanations for similarities and differences.

topics.name: Courses and Teaching
topics.description: Covers teaching, courses, lectures, class assignments, online events, and ways biblical studies is presented to students or public audiences.

topics.name: Textbooks and Teaching Materials
topics.description: Covers textbooks and introductory teaching materials, including how they are written, revised, illustrated, and used to teach biblical studies.

topics.name: Textual Criticism Methods
topics.description: Posts on how textual critics evaluate readings, weigh evidence, identify variants, reconstruct texts, and debate the aims and limits of the discipline.

topics.name: Textual Criticism Overview
topics.description: Posts introducing textual criticism as a field, including why textual variants matter, how textual scholars work, and common questions about the reliability of the New Testament text.

topics.name: Textual Variants
topics.description: Posts on differences among manuscript copies, why variants matter, and how changed wording can affect interpretation of biblical texts.

topics.name: Theologically Significant Variants
topics.description: Posts on textual variants and scribal changes tied to theological controversy, including disputes over Christology, Gnostic interpretations, and emerging proto-orthodox claims.

topics.name: Translation Issues
topics.description: Covers difficult translation decisions, ancient languages, textual bases, inclusive language, misleading translations, and how translation affects interpretation.

topics.name: Trial of Jesus
topics.description: Covers Jesus' trial before Jewish leaders or Pilate, including the charge against him, Gospel portrayals, anti-Judaism, Pilate traditions, and later non-canonical expansions.

topics.name: Development of the Trinity
topics.description: Covers the development of the doctrine of the Trinity, including the Father, Son, Spirit, Nicaea, biblical interpretation, and later Christian theology.

topics.name: Triumph of Christianity
topics.description: Posts about Bart's book The Triumph of Christianity, including writing updates, interviews, lectures, reviews, and discussions of how Christianity spread in the Roman world.

topics.name: Valentinian Gnostics
topics.description: Covers Valentinian Christians, their interpretation of scripture, theology, Gospel of Philip, and disputes with other early Christian groups.

topics.name: Virgin Birth
topics.description: Covers virgin birth traditions, including Matthew and Luke, textual questions, adoptionist Christology, ancient parallels, and historical or theological interpretation.

topics.name: Visionary Experiences
topics.description: Covers visions, group visions, hallucinations, resurrection appearances, and questions about whether visionary experiences can be shared or historically evaluated.

topics.name: Women in Early Christianity
topics.description: Posts on women's roles, leadership, silence, gender hierarchy, and social status in early Christian texts and communities.

topics.name: Writing and Publishing Process
topics.description: Covers writing habits, reading and research methods for books, trade-book production, publishing, titles, agents, reviews, and bringing scholarship to readers.

topics.name: Zealot Hypothesis
topics.description: Posts on claims that Jesus supported armed resistance to Rome, including the temple incident, zealot interpretations, Roman rule, and Bart's critique of the hypothesis.
